content.assessment: This proposal requires high actor capacity and careful sequencing (system-wide reassignment of entry points) that is not clearly matched by current conditions. Structural teacher shortages, low wages, and non-teaching burdens on existing staff constrain the workforce capacity needed to absorb a system-wide restructuring, while the political reaction to even a narrower admissions review indicates high resistance to sequencing a big-bang structural change without a pilot phase.
opportunities: Existing ABC testing infrastructure could be adapted to monitor outcomes of a phased rollout if sequencing were introduced.
strengths: Targets the full selection funnel directly, which aligns with the scale of segregation documented as comparable to Latin American systems.
threats: A minister's narrowly framed admissions-review proposal already triggered partisan conflict and accusations of sweeping transformation, suggesting a full structural reform would face even greater implementation resistance before delivery capacity issues are even addressed.
weaknesses: Teacher shortages and high student-teacher ratios, with teachers already absorbing non-teaching duties, indicate insufficient workforce capacity to manage a disruptive system-wide transition.
No pilot or phased mechanism is described; the proposal implies a single discrete structural shift rather than a testable, containable rollout.
